name: Housing and Credit Counseling, Inc. (Hcci)
description_short: Nonprofit credit and housing counseling organization (now merged with MMI) offering free debt management, homebuyer education, and financial counseling services nationwide.
description_long: Housing and Credit Counseling, Inc. (HCCI) is a nonprofit financial counseling organization that merged with Money Management International (MMI) effective January 1, 2026. MMI is the nation's largest nonprofit credit counseling agency with over 60 years of operation dedicated to helping Americans overcome financial challenges through coaching and consumer advocacy. HCCI/MMI provides comprehensive financial counseling and education services including credit counseling, debt management plans, debt resolution plans, and specialized services such as bankruptcy counseling, homebuyer education, student loan counseling, and disaster recovery assistance. The organization also operates community-specific programs like the Topeka Opportunity to Own (TOTO) program for first-time homebuyers and tenant-landlord counseling in Kansas. Most services are provided at no cost, with a few select low-cost services and optional fees associated with debt management plans; clients may qualify for reduced or waived fees. All counseling is confidential and available 24/7 in English and Spanish with interpreter services for additional languages. The merger with MMI significantly expanded HCCI's digital resources, tools, and nationwide service access while maintaining the localized expertise and compassionate support HCCI was known for. HCCI is well-suited for consumers seeking legitimate nonprofit credit counseling without fees or pressure to enroll in debt products, though the merged entity's scale is considerably larger than HCCI operated independently.
pros: Most counseling and educational services provided completely free of charge
24/7 availability with multiple contact methods (phone, online chat, email)
Services available in English, Spanish, and additional languages via interpreter service
Specialized expertise in bankruptcy counseling, homebuyer education, and disaster recovery
Completely confidential counseling with no credit bureau reporting without permission
Access to free educational resources including budget guides, webinars, and online homebuyer courses
Merged with nation's largest nonprofit credit counselor (MMI) for expanded resources and digital tools
cons: Debt management plans include fees (though may be reduced or waived based on income)
Not a credit repair service and cannot dispute items on credit reports
Recent merger means organizational transition and potential service changes for existing clients
TOTO and tenant-landlord counseling programs appear limited to Kansas communities only
Generic debt management and resolution plans may not address specialized situations like legitimate credit report errors
best_for: Low-income consumers needing free financial counseling without fees or sales pressure
First-time homebuyers seeking HUD-approved homebuyer education courses
Individuals considering bankruptcy who need counseling before filing
Kansas residents seeking homeownership programs or tenant-landlord dispute assistance
diagnosis: HCCI is best for consumers seeking legitimate, free nonprofit financial counseling without pressure to enroll in costly debt solutions. The main caveat is that while they offer debt management plans, they cannot dispute credit report errors or provide credit repair—they provide financial coaching and education to help clients improve their situation over time.
services: Debt management plans (fee-based, may be reduced or waived)
Online financial review and custom debt plans
Tenant-landlord counseling (Kansas communities with grant funding)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Nonprofit credit and housing counseling offering free debt management, homebuyer education, and financial counseling nationwide.
